items.title: Price per square foot for housing
items.scenario: A regression dataset has `list_price` and `living_area_sqft`. Engineers add `price_per_sqft = list_price / living_area_sqft.clip(lower=50)` so linear regression compares homes on a size-normalized basis instead of raw price alone.
items.explanation: Ratios remove confounding scale effects—a $500k mansion and a $500k condo differ in implied value per foot when area varies widely.

items.title: Discount × holiday interaction for retail
items.scenario: A chain predicts daily sales. `promo_depth` alone is weak on regular days but strong on holidays. They add `promo_x_holiday = promo_depth * is_holiday` so linear regression can learn the boosted promo effect on holiday weekends.
items.explanation: Interactions encode conditional effects that additive linear terms cannot represent without explicit cross-columns.

items.title: Session hour and weekend flags for churn
items.scenario: Mobile app logs `last_active_at` timestamps. After `pd.to_datetime`, the team derives `hour`, `is_weekend`, and `days_since_signup` from `(last_active_at - signup_at).days` to feed a logistic model predicting 30-day churn.
items.explanation: Calendar and elapsed-time columns turn opaque timestamps into behavioral rhythms models can separate.

items.title: Customer lifetime aggregates via groupby
items.scenario: Orders table has many rows per `customer_id`. Before modeling churn at customer grain, engineers join `orders.groupby('customer_id').agg(order_count=('order_id','count'), avg_order_value=('amount','mean'))` back to the customer table.
items.explanation: Aggregated derived features summarize history; compute them only from transactions before the prediction cutoff to avoid leakage.
